summary: The Docker build failed because a RUN wget command trying to download a nydus release from GitHub returned a 502 Bad Gateway, causing the build step to exit with code 1 and buildx bake to fail.
root_cause: The Dockerfile RUN wget step for nydus failed with an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway / proxy error, causing the step to exit with code 1 and abort the build.

evidence.quote: #72 0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the downloader (wget) received a 502 response from the remote server when fetching the nydus artifact.
Identifies the Dockerfile line where the failing wget RUN command is defined.
The exact RUN command being executed in the Dockerfile that failed to download the remote release.

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Buildkit reports the wget command failed (exit code 1), which is the direct cause of the build failure.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: The CI step (buildx bake) failed due to the unsuccessful wget process, confirming the build failure surfaced to the workflow.

suggested_fix: Retry the job (this appears to be a transient network/remote-server error). If failures persist, make the Dockerfile download step more resilient (e.g., add retries/backoff to wget or curl, or fetch the nydus artifact from a stable mirror or include it in the build cache) so a transient 502 doesn't fail the entire build.

summary: A functional test failed when pip attempted to install a test package from GitHub: the downloader hit repeated 502 errors from github.com and the pip command exited with a non-zero status.
root_cause: tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] failed because pip could not download https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip due to repeated 502 error responses from github.com, causing an OSError and a non-zero exit code.

evidence.reason: This is the test that failed (appears as the failing test identifier in the log).

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: The pip install attempt failed with an OSError indicating repeated 502 responses from github.com, showing a network/server error while fetching the test package.
The test failed because the pip command exited with a non-zero exit code (the script returned code 1), consistent with the network download error.

suggested_fix: Retry the CI run (this appears to be a transient network/remote-service 502). If failures recur, make the test resilient to remote 502s by retrying the download in the test, mocking the GitHub download, or using a local fixture/archive instead of fetching from github.com.

summary: The CI job failed because one Jest test suite failed: the test file test/config/get.ts threw an error (see stack trace) causing Jest to exit with status 1.
root_cause: A test in test/config/get.ts threw an error (stack trace points to test/config/get.ts:32:38), causing Jest to fail and the overall job to exit with code 1.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Shows that one test suite failed, causing the job to fail.
Stack trace points to the exact test file and line where the error occurred.

evidence.quote: [WARN] Failed to replace env in config: ${ENV_VAR_123}
evidence.reason: A warning about failing to replace an environment variable appeared in the test output and may be related to the failing test's behavior.
pnpm reports the test command exited with an error, confirming the test failure propagated to the job exit.

suggested_fix: Open test/config/get.ts at the reported line (32) and reproduce the failing test locally (run the package's test script or: cross-env NODE_OPTIONS="$NODE_OPTIONS --experimental-vm-modules --disable-warning=ExperimentalWarning --disable-warning=DEP0169" jest test/config/get.ts). If the failure is due to missing environment replacement, set or mock ENV_VAR_123 in the test environment or update the test to handle a missing replacement; then re-run the test suite in CI.
